summary: Executed backend pytest + frontend Playwright validation for phase-aware manual analysis, phase table headings, file type guardrails, and admin default login using public URL.

minor.issue: Existing regression tests in /app/backend/tests/test_platform_core_flows.py fail due outdated assertions expecting non-comparison chart titles even when both phases are provided.

integration_issues.issue: Browser console repeatedly logs Recharts width/height -1 warnings during result rendering, although charts eventually render.

action_items: Update stale assertions in /app/backend/tests/test_platform_core_flows.py for mixed datasets where both phases are present.
Investigate and reduce Recharts responsive container width/height -1 console warnings in manual analysis result rendering.
critical_code_review_comments: compute_analysis switches to explicit before-vs-after mode correctly when both phases exist; requested behavior is implemented.
File upload endpoint correctly rejects non-CSV/XLSX with 400 and explicit error detail.
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST users and analysis records via API/UI during regression runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Requested flows passed end-to-end on public URL. Verify after updating stale backend test assertions and chart container warning behavior.
rca of the issue: Backend test failures are from assertion drift: product now intentionally compares before/after when both phases are supplied, but legacy tests still expect non-comparison titles. Recharts warnings likely occur during initial mount/layout timing before final container sizing.
